refactor: git設定ファイルをXDGディレクトリに統合

~/.gitconfig, ~/.gitconfig-aliases, ~/.gitmessage を
~/.config/git/ 配下に移動してホームディレクトリを整理。

- .gitconfig → .config/git/config
- .gitconfig-aliases → .config/git/config-aliases
- .gitmessage → .config/git/message

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix WCAG AA color-contrast regressions in compliance policy dashboard and compliance wizard (#634)

* Initial plan

* Fix WCAG contrast regressions: replace text-gray-500 with text-gray-400, solid opaque backgrounds, and immediate dark-class seeding

- e2e/helpers/auth.ts: add document.documentElement.classList.add('dark') to
  addInitScript so the dark class is applied before the very first Vue render,
  eliminating the flash-of-light-mode timing window that caused 40 intermittent
  text-gray-400 failures in the compliance-setup-workspace axe scan

- e2e/global-setup.ts: same dark-class-immediate fix for Vite pre-warm visits

- src/components/complianceSetup/JurisdictionPolicyStep.vue: text-gray-500 →
  text-gray-400 (6.93:1 on glass-effect PASS vs 3.64:1 FAIL)

- src/components/complianceSetup/WhitelistEligibilityStep.vue: same fix

- src/components/complianceSetup/ReadinessSummaryStep.vue: replace all
  semi-transparent /10 /20 /30 /50 opacity backgrounds with solid equivalents
  (axe cannot compute contrast through CSS opacity layers); fix text-gray-500 →
  text-gray-400; fix text-yellow-200/80 → text-yellow-200

- src/components/whitelist/PolicySummaryPanel.vue: bg-white/5 → bg-gray-800
  for metric boxes and explanation panel (solid background for reliable contrast)

- src/components/whitelist/WhitelistDetailPanel.vue: dark:text-gray-500 →
  dark:text-gray-400 (3.64:1 FAIL → 6.93:1 PASS in dark mode)

- src/components/whitelist/WhitelistJurisdictionView.vue: text-gray-500 →
  text-gray-400; bg-gray-800/50 → bg-gray-800 in modal detail panels

Co-authored-by: ludovit-scholtz <256357527+ludovit-scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: remove glass-effect/gradient conflicts in ReadinessSummaryStep, use border-only score indicator

- ReadinessSummaryStep.vue readinessScoreClass: replace bg-gradient overlay on glass-effect with a
  border-only indicator (border-2 border-{color}-700) since glass-effect's background shorthand has
  higher CSS specificity than Tailwind @layer utilities and would silently suppress the gradient
- ReadinessSummaryStep.vue deploy-readiness card: remove glass-effect class so the from-green-900
  to-emerald-900 gradient actually renders (no longer overridden by the .dark .glass-effect background rule)
- All changed badge/text combinations verified to meet WCAG AA:
  text-red-300 on bg-red-900: 5.28:1, text-orange-300 on bg-orange-900: 5.56:1,
  text-yellow-300 on bg-yellow-900: 6.58:1, text-blue-300 on bg-blue-900: 5.74:1

Co-authored-by: ludovit-scholtz <256357527+ludovit-scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: .ktmage ツールキットを dotfiles リポジトリに変換

- GNU Stow ベースのシンボリックリンク管理に移行
  - zsh/, git/, mise/, karabiner/ の Stow パッケージを作成
  - vscode/settings.json は手動リンク（パスが特殊なため）
- install.sh を追加（Homebrew, Oh My Zsh, Stow, macOS defaults）
- Brewfile を追加（現在の Homebrew パッケージ一覧）
- macos/defaults.sh を追加（macOS システム設定）
- .zshrc を分割構成に変更（.zsh/path.zsh, aliases.zsh, functions.zsh, keybindings.zsh）
- Git worktree スクリプトを git/ Stow パッケージ内に移動
- エージェント関連を agents/ に集約しプレーン Markdown 化
  - テンプレートエンジン・バージョン管理・生成スクリプトを削除
  - 生成済み最終ファイルのみ保持
- README.md を dotfiles リポジトリ向けに書き換え

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: add workflow_dispatch trigger to release workflow (#24)

Allows manually dispatching the release workflow with a tag input.
The workflow will create and push the tag from the current HEAD, then
proceed with the existing build and GitHub Release creation steps.

A job-level guard (github.actor != 'github-actions[bot]') prevents
the push-tag event (fired by this workflow pushing the tag) from
triggering a duplicate release run.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add text/code file attachment support (#23)

Extend downloadFileAttachments (formerly downloadImageAttachments) to
accept text/* MIME types and 35+ common code/config file extensions
(TypeScript, Python, Go, Rust, Java, shell scripts, SQL, YAML, etc.)
that Discord may classify as application/octet-stream.

- Add TEXT_EXTENSIONS set for extension-based detection
- Add BARE_FILENAMES set for extensionless files (Dockerfile, Makefile, etc.)
- Add isAcceptedFile() predicate combining MIME and extension checks
- Rename function to downloadFileAttachments (deprecated alias kept)
- Update temp file prefix from discord-img- to discord-file-
- Update extension fallback: image→.png, text→.txt
- Update call sites in mention, ask, and chat handlers

Text files dragged onto a Discord message and sent with an @mention
are now forwarded to Copilot as file-path attachments, same as images.
Limits unchanged: 10 MB max, 5 files max.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: heal @github/copilot/definitions/ when missing after --install-links (#22)

* fix: heal @github/copilot/definitions/ when missing after --install-links

npm install -g --install-links uses a stricter tar extractor that fails to
create parent directories before writing files into them, leaving
node_modules/@github/copilot/definitions/ absent and causing
TAR_ENTRY_ERROR ENOENT warnings.

The postinstall script now detects the missing directory and re-installs
@github/copilot at the exact pinned version using --no-install-links,
ensuring the YAML agent definitions are present. Falls back to a warning
with remediation instructions if the re-install itself fails.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: bump version to 1.0.14

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: bump version to 1.0.13 (#21)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Continue long AI responses across multiple Discord messages (#20)

Replace truncateForDiscord() with chunkForDiscord() that splits responses
at paragraph/newline/word boundaries instead of silently truncating.

- Add chunkForDiscord(text, maxLen=1990) in src/copilot.ts with
  line-by-line code-fence tracking: closes an open fence at the split
  point and reopens it (with the same language tag) in the next chunk
- Remove truncateForDiscord export entirely
- Update all 9 handler call sites to send first chunk via editReply/reply
  and subsequent chunks via followUp (ephemeral where appropriate) or reply

Handlers updated: mention, ask, chat (3 send sites), history, plan,
agent, servers, model, workspace

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: fallback through all LRC candidates on fetch failure

Previously, only the first synced candidate was tried. If its LRC file
was missing (404) or had invalid path chars like null bytes (400), the
entire repo was skipped.

Now iterates all candidates (synced first, preserving order) until one
succeeds. Skips paths containing null bytes. Only gives up when all
candidates fail.

Recovers ~24% of repos that were previously lost.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: TJDominos <tjluckydominos@gmail.com>

feat: extract Discord embed content in message link resolution (#18)

Sonarr and other bot integrations send notifications as Discord embeds
(rich objects with title, description, fields, footer) rather than plain
msg.content. The previous implementation fell back to '(no text content)'
for these messages, making linked context useless.

Changes:
- Add formatEmbeds() helper in resolveMessageLinks.ts that serialises
  msg.embeds[] into quoted plain-text lines (author, title, description,
  fields, footer)
- Use flatMap().split('\n') to ensure multiline field values are each
  individually prefixed with '>' (caught by adversarial review)
- Body assembly now combines msg.content + embed blocks; falls back to
  '(no text content)' only when both are empty
- Multiple embeds separated by '> ---' divider

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- withAuth() now seeds theme=dark to ensure axe scans run in dark mode (product's primary design mode)
- global-setup.ts warmup also seeds dark theme for consistent compilation
- Fix ComplianceLaunchConsole: text-gray-400/500 → text-gray-300, semi-transparent badges → solid
- Fix TeamWorkspaceView: text-gray-400/500 → text-gray-300, bg-gray-800/60 stat counters → bg-gray-800
- Fix WorkItemCard: priority badges (bg-red-900/40 text-red-300) → solid (bg-red-800 text-white)
- Fix ComplianceSetupWorkspace: dark:text-gray-400 → dark:text-gray-200 in cards, step labels text-gray-500 → text-gray-300
- Fix Settings: inputs bg-white/10 text-gray-900 → bg-gray-700 text-white (axe can measure solid bg)
- Fix BusinessCommandCenter: selects bg-white/10 → bg-gray-700, text-gray-400 labels → text-gray-300

commit.message:
chore: bump version to 1.0.10 (#15)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: resolve Discord message links as context in prompts (#14)

When a user pastes a Discord message URL in any prompt, the bot now
fetches that message's content and prepends it as quoted context before
sending to Copilot.

- Add src/utils/resolveMessageLinks.ts: parses discord.com/channels URLs,
  checks requester permissions via permissionsFor() (prevents reading
  channels the user can't see), fetches message content, and builds a
  quoted context block prepended to the original prompt
- Wire into handleMention (mention + free-channel messages)
- Wire into /ask and /chat slash command handlers
- In /chat, deferReply() is called before resolveMessageLinks() to stay
  within Discord's 3-second interaction response window

Works for any channel the bot has read access to regardless of
DISCORD_FREE_CHANNELS — passive one-time fetch, not active monitoring.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: initial resonance lyrics ecosystem

- tools/: Rust crawler (crawl + build subcommands)
  - LRCHub GraphQL crawl with incremental early termination
  - 256-shard JSON output (SHA-256 based)
  - SQLite build + zstd compression
  - Language detection (ja/ko/zh/en)
  - Dirty shard tracking (only write changed files)

- .github/workflows/crawl.yml: daily incremental + weekly full
- .github/workflows/build.yml: SQLite + delta + Release

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(security): pin undici CVEs and validate workspace path (#13)

* fix(deps): pin undici to ^6.24.0 via overrides to fix 4 CVEs

discord.js 14.25.1 pulls in undici <=6.21.3 which has 4 known
vulnerabilities (3 moderate, 1 high):
- GHSA-g9mf-h72j-4rw9: unbounded decompression chain
- GHSA-f269-vfmq-vjvj: WebSocket 64-bit length overflow
- GHSA-2mjp-6q6p-2qxm: HTTP request/response smuggling
- GHSA-vrm6-8vpv-qv8q: unbounded memory in WebSocket deflate

Added npm overrides to force undici@^6.24.0 (first patched release).
npm audit now reports 0 vulnerabilities.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(security): validate and canonicalize workspace path in setSessionWorkingDir

Previously the workspace option accepted any string, which could be
used to pass path traversal sequences or symlinks that retarget after
validation. Fixes:

- Reject empty strings and null bytes early
- Resolve via fs.realpathSync.native() to follow all symlinks to the
  real canonical path (path.resolve alone does not dereference symlinks)
- Reject nonexistent paths and paths pointing to files
- Store the canonical absolute path, not the user-supplied string
- Surface meaningful error messages back to the Discord user in /chat

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: patch @github/copilot-sdk session.js for ESM vscode-jsonrpc import (#12)

@github/copilot-sdk v0.1.32 dist/session.js imports 'vscode-jsonrpc/node'
without the .js extension. Node 22's strict ESM resolver can't find the
file because vscode-jsonrpc v8.x has no package exports map. The sibling
file client.js already uses the correct 'vscode-jsonrpc/node.js'.

Add a postinstall script (dist/scripts/patch-deps.cjs) that fixes the
import string in place after npm installs all dependencies. The patch
is idempotent and non-fatal if the file is missing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: include ai-assistant.service in npm package (#11)

Removed *.service from .npmignore so the systemd service template is
included in the published package. install-service was failing with
'Service template not found' after npm install.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: anchor .npmignore patterns to root to prevent dist/scripts exclusion (#10)

The unanchored 'scripts/' pattern was also matching 'dist/scripts/',
causing dist/scripts/register-commands.js to be omitted from the npm
package. 'ai-assistant register' failed with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND.

Fix: prefix src/ and scripts/ with / to anchor to package root.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
